description: Monitor battery voltage, battery level percentage, and external power supply state transmitted by GPS tracking hardware, and emit alerts when power conditions threaten continuous device operation (low battery, power cut, power restoration).

actors.name: GPS Device
actors.description: Reports battery voltage, battery level, and power supply state in position transmissions

actors.name: Alarm Event Handler
actors.description: Detects power-related alarm codes in positions and generates events

actors.name: Fleet User
actors.description: Receives power alerts to arrange battery replacement or investigate tampering

fields.name: battery_level
fields.description: Battery charge level as a percentage (0–100%)

fields.name: external_power_voltage
fields.description: External (vehicle) power supply voltage in volts

fields.name: alarm
fields.description: Comma-separated alarm codes in the position that may include power-related codes: low_battery, low_power, power_off, power_on, power_cut, power_restored

rules: Power alarm codes are embedded in the position alarm attribute by the device; each code generates a separate alert event
low_battery indicates the internal battery is below a device-defined threshold; devices typically decide the threshold in firmware
power_cut indicates the vehicle external power supply has been disconnected (possible tampering or vehicle power failure)
power_restored indicates external power has been reconnected after a power_cut event
power_off and power_on reflect transitions detected by the device in the main power rail
Battery and power voltage readings are stored on every position and can be charted over time to identify degradation trends
Power alert events follow the same alarm processing pipeline as other alarm types (see device-alarm-notifications); duplicate suppression applies

given: position alarm attribute contains a power-related alarm code

any: alarm code was not present in the previous position

then.description: Power alarm event recorded with type = alarm, alarm_type = power alarm code, device_id, position_id

power_alarm_generated.result: Power alert event stored; notification handlers dispatch alerts to subscribed users

given: position includes battery_level or battery_voltage attributes

then.description: Battery level percentage stored on position

battery_data_recorded.result: Battery telemetry captured on position record; available for trend analysis and reporting

given: position does not include any power or battery attributes
no_power_data.result: No power events generated for this position; device does not report power telemetry

errors.message: The device referenced does not exist

events.name: device.power_alarm
events.description: Device has reported a power-related condition (low battery, power cut, power restored)

related.description: Power alarms are processed through the same alarm event pipeline
Power telemetry and alarm codes arrive as position attributes
Power cut may correlate with device going offline shortly after
